steps.title: Set up the relationships and work backwards from the known graduate count
steps.content:
Let $U$ = undergraduates, $G$ = graduates, $P$ = postdoctoral. The given relationships are:
$$G = 0.26U$$
$$U = 72.5P$$
We know $G = 3{,}770$. Working backwards from this known value, we can find both $U$ and $P$.

steps.title: Find the number of undergraduate students
Find the number of postdoctoral students and calculate the sum
